factors.name: Fed Policy Expectations
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh (new Fed Chair implied context) and hawkish ECB member Schnabel both signaling rates may stay higher for longer; Warsh seen as hawkish on inflation trajectory. No imminent rate cuts priced in.

factors.name: Energy / Geopolitical Shock
factors.summary: US-Iran strikes over weekend resumed, then ceasefire talks resumed for Tuesday in Qatar. Oil jumped modestly then unwound; Strait of Hormuz risk remains elevated but not acute. Short-term relief bid but fragile.

factors.name: Real Yields & Duration Risk
factors.summary: Higher real rates (via hawkish Fed rhetoric) compress long-duration growth multiples. Tech mega-caps (NDX core holdings) particularly vulnerable to real yield steepening; 10Y Treasury likely to remain sticky.

factors.name: Sovereign De-Dollarization Trend
factors.summary: News reports $29T in sovereign capital pivoting away from dollar, toward gold and private assets; Bolivia abandoning 15-year peg signals FX instability. Structural dollar weakness potential but distant catalyst.

factors.name: Leverage Concerns in Equities
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports leverage fueling US stock rally is now a growing source of unease; risk-off or volatility spike could force deleveraging and hurt momentum stocks (NDX-heavy).

factors.name: Mega-Cap Tech Earnings / AI Capex Narrative
factors.summary: Apple WWDC 2026 shows 'material positive reset' of AI strategy per headline; hyperscaler capex appetite remains strong (Nvidia, cloud providers). No near-term earnings surprise catalyst visible in news window.

factors.name: China Industrial Demand & Margin Pressure
factors.summary: China industrial profits +21% YoY in May but growth slowing; auto maker profits down 19.8% despite export strength = acute margin compression. Negative for semiconductor / supply chain demand outlook.

factors.name: Data Releases This Week
factors.summary: US NFP, ISM Mfg PMI, CPI flash data due early week; no surprises yet. RBA minutes Tuesday could reignite EM FX volatility. ECB inflation data also watched—no hawkish shock yet but Schnabel warning suggests tightening bias.

factors.name: Crypto Correlation & Risk Sentiment
factors.summary: Bitcoin fell below $60k (weekend), signaling capitulation but also risk-off mood. BTC-NQ correlation elevated; weak crypto tone suggests defensive posture favors lower multiple growth trades.

factors.name: Technical Price Action
factors.summary: NAS100 down -0.75% in 4h and -0.58% in 24h—modest downside, not panicked yet. Intraday weakness into close Sunday, consistent with profit-taking post-weekend geopolitical relief bid unwind.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is caught between hawkish Fed messaging (Warsh, Schnabel) that keeps real yields elevated and supported, versus short-term relief from US-Iran ceasefire talks resuming. The index is down modestly on the session but not breaking key support; sentiment is cautious given leverage concerns and China growth headwinds. The dominant story remains that rate expectations are sticky-high, compressing long-duration growth multiples, while the geopolitical relief was priced in Friday and has partially unwound. This is a classic Phase 2 chop: move didn't extend far but buyers are not aggressive ahead of major data (NFP/CPI early week).
catalysts_ahead: US Dallas Fed Mfg Index (Monday)
US NFP (June, Friday) — major risk event
US ISM Mfg PMI (likely Tuesday/Wednesday) — Fed signal
ECB flash CPI (likely Tuesday) — Schnabel hawkish pressure
RBA Minutes (Tuesday) — EM currency volatility trigger
China NBS Mfg PMI (Tuesday) — growth signal
US-Iran Doha talks (Tuesday) — geopolitical de-escalation check
screening.risk_warning: If US-Iran talks collapse and oil spikes >10%, or if NFP surprise hawkish (e.g. >250k), NDX could break 28,650 support and test 28,300. Conversely, if Fed guidance softens or inflation data surprises dovish (CPI <2.5% YoY), real yields could compress and unlock a 1-2% rally. Leverage unwind risk is real on any volatility shock >2% down day.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early phase 2; avoid chasing lower here. Wait for retest of 29,200–29,300 resistance to short, or hold and buy any dip to 28,900 support only on material dovish data surprise (CPI <2.5% or Fed pivot signal). NFP Friday is the key catalyst—do not size into event risk.

factors.name: AI capex and systemic risk warnings
factors.summary: BIS warned that AI investment surge poses systemic risk due to 'enormous debt and highly leveraged nonbank structures that can rapidly unwind.' This echoes dot-com/railway mania concerns and directly challenges the capex narrative underpinning mega-cap valuations.

factors.name: Excessive data center power/heat stress
factors.summary: Multiple reports flagging severe weather, grid strain, and insurance/repair cost risks for AI data centers. This introduces operational and margin pressure on hyperscaler capex ROI assumptions, undermining forward guidance credibility.

factors.name: Geopolitical relief (US-Iran ceasefire)
factors.summary: US and Iran agreed to halt attacks and resume Doha talks Tuesday; oil pared gains and fell below $70. Lower energy risk typically supports growth equities and reduces inflation hedging demand, benefiting duration-sensitive mega-caps.

factors.name: Crypto ETF outflows and crypto weakness
factors.summary: Spot Bitcoin ETFs on track for worst month on record ($4B+ outflows); BTC holding below $60k. Crypto weakness historically signals risk-off repositioning and reduced appetite for high-beta, long-duration tech.

factors.name: Near-term data calendar intensity
factors.summary: Week includes US NFP (Friday), ISM Manufacturing PMI, multiple inflation prints (EZ Flash CPI, Swiss CPI, French HICP). Holiday-shortened week (US closes early Thursday, Friday). Volatility likely concentrated into these releases.

factors.name: China stimulus (PBOC easing signals)
factors.summary: PBOC set overnight reverse repo rate at 1.25% (below forecasts), signaling de facto rate cut. China's industrial profits up 21% in May but domestic demand lags. Mixed signal: easing tone positive for risk, but weak demand growth raises recession tail risk.

factors.name: Semiconductor positive catalysts
factors.summary: Samsung and SK Hynix $518B chip fab investment, South Korea $650B AI/chip mega-plan, Baidu AI chip unit (Kunlunxin) targeting $50B Hong Kong IPO. Supply chain demand signals remain robust despite capex quality concerns.

factors.name: Tech regulation/antitrust headwinds
factors.summary: Australia expanding regulator power over Big Tech; China blacklisting Japanese tech entities (Mitsubishi, Komatsu, Fujitsu units). Regulatory pressure mounting on global tech conglomerates with NAS100 heavy exposure.

factors.name: Dollar stability, real yields elevated
factors.summary: DXY flat at 101.35; USD remains bid despite Hormuz relief. Real yields (TIPS) still elevated, which caps multiple expansion for long-duration Nasdaq growth stocks. No fresh Fed dovish signal in news window.

factors.name: Recent price action (4h -0.75%, 24h -0.58%)
factors.summary: Index down modestly overnight despite geopolitical relief. Suggests sellers held on Iran ceasefire and AI concerns are overriding near-term safety bid. Move modest but directionally weak.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq100 is caught between conflicting forces: geopolitical relief (US-Iran ceasefire) and oil decline support risk appetite, while BIS systemic AI capex warnings, data center stress concerns, and record Bitcoin ETF outflows signal underlying risk-off repositioning in long-duration tech. Semiconductor capex announcements (Samsung/SK Hynix/Baidu) provide a positive counterweight, but their credibility is being tested by questions about hyperscaler ROI and the leverage funding them. With a data-heavy week ahead (NFP, ISM, multi-region CPI) and real yields still elevated, the index faces competing narratives until inflation/Fed expectations clarify. Current -0.75% 4h decline suggests intraday sellers are cautious despite headline relief.
catalysts_ahead: US NFP (Friday) — Goldman expects 130k headline with 40k World Cup distortion, implying 90k underlying (soft signal for September cut if realized)
US ISM Manufacturing PMI (Monday/Tue) — manufacturing weakness would support lower rates narrative
EZ Flash CPI (Tuesday) — ECB board member Schnabel called for further hikes despite Hormuz relief; hot inflation could reinforce hawkish tone
RBA Minutes (Tuesday) — Australian policy stance amid global uncertainty
PBOC/China Manufacturing PMI (Tuesday) — domestic demand weakness vs. easing bias
US Dallas Fed Manufacturing (Monday) — early June regional activity read
UK GDP Final (Tuesday) — Q1 growth revision
Mega-cap earnings announcements (dates not specified in window, but Apple, Microsoft, Google, Nvidia commentary on capex ROI critical to Nasdaq narrative)
screening.risk_warning: Geopolitical escalation (US-Iran talks collapse) could trigger an oil spike and flight-to-safety bid, reversing bearish crypto/AI capex narrative. Conversely, surprise hawkish CPI or Fed communication would pressure duration multiples further. Mega-cap earnings disappointment on guidance would amplify AI capex skepticism and trigger 2-3% selloff. Failure to hold 29000 support would signal deeper pullback toward 28700-28500.
screening.entry_guidance: Phase 2 / move modest and inconclusive. Avoid chasing downside; wait for clearer setup at 29000 support or hold for pullback/retest before committing. Data risk skews toward volatility into NFP Friday. Consider small longs only if 29250 resistance breaks with volume, or add to shorts on rollover through 28900.

factors.name: Geopolitical De-escalation (US-Iran)
factors.summary: US and Iran agreed to halt attacks ahead of peace talks; Strait of Hormuz traffic recovering. Risk-off flows reversing, supporting equity appetite. Brent Oil fell sharply last week easing inflation concerns.

factors.name: Fed Hawkish Repricing (Post-FOMC)
factors.summary: FOMC hawkish dot plot triggered repricing: 32 bps of tightening priced by year-end, 62% probability of hike in September. Stronger USD (DXY near YTD highs) and higher real yields compress growth multiples in Nasdaq-heavy long-duration tech.

factors.name: Tech Sector Momentum Collapse
factors.summary: Magnificent Seven in correction territory; 4h change -0.75%, 24h change -0.58%. Philly Semiconductor Index slumped; chip sector weakness spreading despite Micron beat. S&P 500 underperformed equally-weighted by 350 bps last week.

factors.name: AI/Semiconductor Capex Optimism
factors.summary: South Korea announced $880B AI chips & data center mega-project (Samsung, SK Hynix); Chinese robotics startups valued at $2.9B+. Hyperscaler capex still robust despite recent volatility, supporting semiconductor long-term demand narrative.

factors.name: Dollar Strength & Real Yields
factors.summary: USD Index at YTD highs; real yields elevated due to hawkish Fed expectations. Inverse relationship: stronger USD and higher real rates compress NDX duration valuations and reduce relative attractiveness of growth equities vs. bonds.

factors.name: Tech Fund Outflows
factors.summary: Record outflows from tech funds as investors trim equity positions per recent news flow. Sector rotation away from mega-cap tech toward defensive names and value, pressuring NDX composition.

factors.name: Upcoming NFP & JOLTS Data
factors.summary: JOLTS job openings expected to decline slightly in May (Tuesday 14:00 GMT); US NFP for June due Thursday. These releases could re-price rate expectations; until then, forward guidance is murky.

factors.name: Apple Antitrust & China Headwinds
factors.summary: Apple accused India of 'copy-pasting' rivals' claims in antitrust probe; Apple exploring China memory suppliers amid chip crunch. Single mega-cap AAPL can move NDX 2-3%; regulation and supply-chain friction add uncertainty.

factors.name: Macro De-risking & Lower Oil
factors.summary: Oil price pullback easing inflation fears; gold down 1.4% on de-escalation. Lower commodity costs may cool near-term inflation print, potentially opening door to dovish Fed re-pricing if data cooperates.

factors.name: BIS Warning on AI Valuation Froth
factors.summary: Bank for International Settlements warns AI frenzy could trigger stock-market slump; highlights rich valuations, investor complacency, and circular financing risks. Macro headwind to stretched growth multiples.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is caught between conflicting forces: geopolitical relief and lower oil prices offer tentative risk-on support, but the Fed's June hawkish repricing (62% odds of September hike, stronger USD, elevated real yields) is compressing growth valuations and fueling tech sector outflows. The Magnificent Seven correction and record tech fund redemptions signal momentum loss. Although hyperscaler capex (Samsung, SK Hynix mega-projects) and AI demand remain constructive longer-term, near-term technicals show weakness (-0.75% 4h, -0.58% 24h) in a Phase 2 pullback that has not yet found stable support. This is a consolidation zone with 'lower tech multiple risk' prevailing until NFP/JOLTS data (Thu–Tue) reprices rate expectations.
catalysts_ahead: JOLTS Job Openings (May) – Tuesday 14:00 GMT; expected decline
US NFPR (June) – Thursday; markets pricing 29% July hike odds, 62% Sept hike odds
US Manufacturing PMI (June) – Wednesday
Eurozone CPI (June final) – Wednesday; ECB Sintra forum signals
UK Final GDP (Q1) – Tuesday
FOMC minutes (if any surprises on interim messaging)
Major mega-cap earnings if scheduled in next 72h (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA guidance)
screening.risk_warning: If NFP comes in hotter-than-expected (>250k jobs), expect sharp Fed re-pricing higher, potential 50 bps repricing, further USD strength, and Nasdaq selloff. Conversely, if NFP/CPI surprise dovish (job misses, inflation cools), real yields could plunge, triggering fast reversal and bullish momentum back above 29500. Geopolitical escalation (renewed US-Iran tensions) could whipsaw commodities and risk sentiment. Tech mega-cap earnings misses or guidance downgrades (Amazon, Apple, NVIDIA) in coming weeks would validate current weakness.
screening.entry_guidance: Avoid chasing shorts on intraday momentum; this is Phase 2 correction with support/resistance zones not yet confirmed. Wait for either a close above 29500 (bullish confirmation) or a break-and-hold below 28900 (bearish continuation). Short-term traders should wait for pullback to 29300–29350 support before considering fresh shorts; longs should wait for close above 29500 before re-entering. NFP Thursday is the next major inflection point—cap leverage until after that release.

factors.name: Consumer Cyclical Surge
factors.summary: Amazon (AMZN) +4.89% and Tesla (TSLA) +4.52% leading charge in consumer cyclicals, signaling risk-on appetite and dip-buying demand in mega-cap tech. Palantir +4% on Nvidia sovereign AI pact further supports tech strength.

factors.name: Geopolitical De-Escalation
factors.summary: U.S.-Iran ceasefire agreement halts mutual attacks; oil near $70 and easing 'war premium' from commodities. Removal of tail-risk geopolitical premium supports growth equities and reduces defensive positioning.

factors.name: Fed Independence Upheld
factors.summary: Supreme Court 5-4 ruling prevents Trump firing of Fed Governor Lisa Cook, reinforcing central bank independence and reducing political risk overhang. Markets have priced this relatively smoothly.

factors.name: Semiconductor Headwinds
factors.summary: Semiconductor sector faces 'notable challenges' per market snapshot; memory pricing tug-of-war between suppliers and megacap spenders reported. Apple, Xbox, Amazon announced pricing hikes due to chip costs, pressuring near-term capex narratives.

factors.name: Fed Policy Patience Signaled
factors.summary: Michael Darda (Roth Capital) noted Fed will remain 'patient'; softer US PCE inflation repriced hike expectations downward per Deutsche Bank. Lower real yields environment supports long-duration growth tech.

factors.name: Freight & Cyclical Recovery Signal
factors.summary: US flatbed spot prices +41% YTD with 24 consecutive weeks of gains, signaling inflection in freight cycle and potential economic acceleration. 'Very good sign' per analysts of macro improvement.

factors.name: Comcast Spin-Off (CMCSA)
factors.summary: Comcast +9% (best day in 18 years) on NBCUniversal/Sky spinoff announcement; positive for that name but not a broad NDX catalyst. M&A activity elevated but stock-specific.

factors.name: China Slowdown & Yen Weakness
factors.summary: China industrial profit growth slowed to 21.1% YoY in May; JPY hit 40-year low at ~162 vs. USD. Yen weakness on BOJ inaction risks carry unwind, and China slowdown pressures tech export demand.

factors.name: AI Capex & Hyperscaler Demand Debate
factors.summary: Morgan Stanley and Bernstein debate peak AI capex; Meta-Google limit suggests pricing tug-of-war. Heatwaves raising AI data center risks (grid strain, insurance costs). Mixed signals on forward capex sustainability.

factors.name: Technical Setup (NQ Repair, Not Breakout)
factors.summary: Per Nasdaq analysis, 'NQ repair is constructive' but '29,760 still matters' and 'buyers have not yet confirmed breakout.' Higher value migration but 30-minute chart shows only mild bullish bias, not conviction.

factors.name: Strategy (MSTR) Bitcoin Framework
factors.summary: MSTR approved $1.25B potential Bitcoin sales, raised STRC dividend to 12%, authorized $2B buybacks. Positive for capital management but reflects liquidity/monetization need, mixed BTC sentiment.

factors.name: SpaceX Nasdaq-100 Entry (Fast-Track)
factors.summary: SpaceX to join NAS100 via fast-track framework, triggering 'huge ETF buying demand' per Bloomberg. Musk's flagship company entering mega-cap index adds structural bid for Nasdaq-100.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq-100 is in mild Phase 2 recovery mode after last week's selloff, buoyed by geopolitical de-escalation (U.S.-Iran ceasefire), mega-cap tech dip-buying (Amazon, Tesla, Palantir surging 4–5%), and softer inflation narrative supporting Fed patience. Consumer cyclicals leading and freight cycle inflection signal macro resilience. However, semiconductor sector pressure, China slowdown, and technical analysis show buyers have not yet achieved confirmed breakout above 29,760—this is repair, not breakout. Fed patience is supportive of multiples, but upcoming data (CPI, PCE risk) and hyperscaler capex guidance remain critical wildcards.
catalysts_ahead: US inflation data (PCE, core PCE) — next major test of Fed-patient narrative; any surprise risks real-yield repricing
ECB Forum Sintra (this week) — Kevin Warsh and Christine Lagarde remarks could shift USD/EUR and yield volatility
FOMC rhetoric / Fed Speakers — any hawkish surprise would compress tech multiples
Q2 mega-cap earnings guidance (NVDA, MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL in coming weeks) — capex commentary critical
China data (industrial production, consumption) — tracks NDX international revenue exposure
Weekly jobless claims, NFP preview — labor market data impacts Fed-rate outlook
screening.risk_warning: Semiconductor weakness and memory pricing deflation could trigger broader tech margin concerns if not contained. China slowdown + JPY carry unwind could spark risk-off repricing. Fed pivot to hawkishness (if inflation re-accelerates) would rapidly compress high-multiple growth tech. Technical failure to sustain above 29,760 invalidates bullish setup and could see retest to 29,400–29,500. SpaceX inclusion euphoria may already be priced in.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 (underway, not extended). Wait for retest of 29,670 support or consolidation around 29,800–29,900 before adding; do not chase extended move above 30,000 without fresh bullish catalyst. If breaks below 29,670, drop to SELL with 29,400 next target.

factors.name: Mega-cap tech single-name strength
factors.summary: GOOG +4.5%, AMZN +3.9%, TSLA +5.8% leading the move; Alphabet joins Dow today, signaling index validation. However, MSFT -0.6% and AAPL -0.9% show selective leadership, not uniform strength.

factors.name: AI/Cloud capex narrative intact
factors.summary: JPMorgan backs crypto bill (indirectly supports fintech/AI infrastructure), Rocket Lab-Iridium M&A signals space-tech momentum. No negative capex guidance from hyperscalers visible in news window.

factors.name: Fed independence reinforced (Lisa Cook ruling)
factors.summary: Supreme Court blocks Trump firing of Fed Governor Cook, protecting central bank independence. Market initially neutral; Trump signals he'll pursue alternative action. Reduces near-term policy uncertainty but doesn't shift rate expectations materially.

factors.name: Fed hawkishness under Warsh
factors.summary: Citadel Securities warns investors are underestimating Warsh's inflation-fighting resolve and likely drag on risk assets. Bloomberg notes June payrolls could boost rate-hike bets. This contradicts earlier 'less hawkish' narrative and threatens growth multiples.

factors.name: Geopolitical de-escalation (US-Iran deal)
factors.summary: US and Iran reach agreement to halt attacks; talks resume Tuesday in Qatar. Oil prices rise modestly (WTI +$1.78 to $71.02) but remain below $71.09 resistance. Reduces tail risk for growth stocks but not transformational.

factors.name: Semiconductor sector under pressure
factors.summary: NVDA and Micron both testing/breaking key support levels; NVDA tests 100-day MA at $196.36, declines toward 200-day MA at $190.72. Suggests internal weakness despite broad NDX strength—potential red flag for sector consolidation.

factors.name: Upcoming macro calendar: NFP Thursday
factors.summary: Kalshi traders give under 60% odds that US adds >100k jobs (vs Dow expectation of 118k+), signaling subdued labor data risk. Event is 4 days away; high uncertainty caps conviction.

factors.name: Treasury yields and real rates
factors.summary: No explicit 10-year or real yield data in this window. EURUSD breaks above 200-hour MA at 1.14236, suggesting modest USD softness. Without yield anchor, growth-multiple risk remains latent.

factors.name: Month-end/quarter-end flows
factors.summary: Investinglive notes 'month-end/quarter-end flows' may be driving selective Nasdaq strength. This typically induces chop and reversion after repositioning clears.

factors.name: Comcast spinoff M&A hype
factors.summary: Comcast +9% on NBCUniversal/Sky spinoff announcement; Charter bonds see record moves on speculation of consolidation. Broadens M&A cycle narrative but tangential to core NDX holdings.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is in the early-to-middle stages of a consumer cyclical / mega-cap AI-leverage rally, with GOOG, AMZN, TSLA leading and AAPL/MSFT lagging. The move is +1.53% in 24h and +0.78% in 4h, suggesting phase-2 momentum (underway, not fresh). However, confluence of tailwinds (geopolitical de-escalation, Alphabet Dow entry, continued cloud/AI narrative) collides with headwinds: Warsh Fed signaling hawkishness, semiconductor sector soft, and upcoming NFP Friday risking disappointment. Lisa Cook Supreme Court ruling removes one policy overhang but doesn't change rate trajectory. Selective leadership (GOOG +4.5% vs MSFT -0.6%) hints at market picking winners in crowded AI race, typical of late-phase advance.
catalysts_ahead: Thursday 2026-07-03: US June Nonfarm Payrolls (Kalshi traders suggest downside miss vs 118k expectations)
This week: US-Iran talks resume Tuesday in Qatar (geopolitical risk management)
Next week: ECB President Lagarde comments at Sintra symposium (may cap EUR upside, signal policy divergence)
Ongoing: NVDA/MSFT/AAPL earnings season tracking and guidance on capex (critical for NDX rerating)
FOMC date unknown in window but likely within 4 weeks—rate path critical to tech multiple sustainability
screening.risk_warning: If NFP comes in significantly below 100k jobs, or Fed Chair Warsh delivers hawkish tone at next appearance, tech multiples compress sharply. Semiconductor weakness (NVDA, Micron breaking support) could cascade into broader semiconductor ETF (SMH/SOXX) selloff, dragging NDX. Geopolitical truce is fragile—any US-Iran escalation re-ignites oil/inflation risk and forces Warsh into tighter policy. Month-end flows reversing Friday/early next week could unwind selective leadership (GOOG strength) quickly.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in phase 2; +1.5% in 24h already captured. Consider nibbling on any pullback to 29400–29500 support with tight stops (29200), or wait for consolidation/retest of recent highs before new longs. Avoid chasing at 30k resistance—higher probability reversion play is to scale in on weakness into NFP volatility.

factors.name: Tesla mega-cap leadership
factors.summary: Tesla surged 8% on self-driving tech update (MarketWatch, 21:24), significantly outperforming and acting as NQ leading indicator. Single mega-cap moves 2-3% of index.

factors.name: Risk-on rotation post-Iran deal
factors.summary: US-Iran ceasefire agreement halted geopolitical premium, oil prices stabilized above $70, reducing safe-haven bid and boosting equity risk appetite. S&P 500 closed near session highs (+1.17%).

factors.name: Real yields and 10Y trajectory
factors.summary: US 10-year Treasury yields steady at ~4.37% (no major catalyst). Fed hike bets fading (money markets pricing <34bps tightening in 2026 per FXStreet). Neutral-to-slightly-dovish bias limits duration headwinds.

factors.name: Dollar strength easing
factors.summary: US Dollar Index weakening for third consecutive session as Fed hike expectations fade. Scotiabank: DXY 'easing' with swaps now pricing <20bps by September. Weaker USD tailwind for NQ international revenue exposure.

factors.name: Semiconductor volatility and divergence
factors.summary: Rare widening gap: semiconductor stocks +80% YTD but Magnificent Seven in correction territory (MarketWatch, 20:08). Micron post-earnings rally nearly gone; SMCI raided in Taiwan on chip-smuggling probe, down -5.7%.

factors.name: Mega-cap vs Dow divergence warning
factors.summary: Dow posting record closes while Nasdaq lagging; this divergence historically precedes bear market with 67% probability (MarketWatch, 19:52). Suggests leadership fragility in tech.

factors.name: Antitrust and regulatory risk
factors.summary: Supreme Court ruled Trump can fire FTC commissioners (overturning 91-year precedent). While initially regulatory-positive, uncertainty around enforcement targeting Big Tech remains; Fed independence also tested (split ruling).

factors.name: 24h momentum continuation
factors.summary: NAS100 up +1.72% over 24h, near session highs. S&P 500 broke above 100-hour MA at 7,420.76, though still below 200-hour MA at 7,462.30, signaling move in progress but not yet extended.

factors.name: Bitcoin weakness and crypto correlation risk
factors.summary: BTC near $60K with put-call ratio at 1-year high; prediction markets betting drop to $55K (CoinDesk/Cointelegraph). MSTR +12-14% today, but broader crypto winter risk and ETF outflows suggest weakening risk-on conviction.

factors.name: Upcoming US jobs data (Thursday NFP)
factors.summary: NFP due Thursday 6/30 or early 7/1; market repricing Fed terminal rates on labor data. No fresh employment data yet in window, but this is immediate catalyst risk capping conviction.

key_levels.resistance: 29900 (session high entry), 30000 (round psychological level, prior range top)

screening.narrative: NAS100 extended gains into session close (+1.72% 24h, +0.16% 4h) on risk-on sentiment following US-Iran ceasefire, Tesla mega-cap leadership (+8%), and fading Fed hawkishness (rate-hike expectations compressed to <20bps by Sep). However, widening gap between semiconductor strength and Magnificent Seven weakness, plus a rare Dow-outperformance signal, warns of near-term consolidation or pullback. Move is already underway (Phase 2), with S&P 500 back above 100-hour MA but still below 200-hour. Upcoming US NFP (Thursday) is immediate overhead risk.
catalysts_ahead: US NFP (nonfarm payrolls) — Thursday 6/30 or early 7/1; critical Fed repricing trigger
Chinese June PMIs — Tuesday 6/30 Asia session; growth/tech demand signal
RBA Minutes — Tuesday 6/30 Asia session; BoP strength implications for AUD, risk sentiment
Fed Beige Book or speaker commentary — potential verbal surprise on rate path
Earnings season continuation: no mega-cap earnings imminent in next 48h
screening.risk_warning: Move is already in Phase 2; late entrants risk pullback if NFP surprises hot (Fed repricing) or if the Dow–Nasdaq divergence warning materializes into broader tech selloff. Semiconductor divergence is live and could trigger sector rotation out of mega-cap growth. BTC weakness and crypto winter sentiment could flip risk-off without fresh regulatory catalyst. Iran ceasefire could reverse if tensions reignite or negotiations collapse.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in progress; wait for pullback to 29600–29700 support zone before adding longs, or trail stops tightly on rips above 29900 resistance. Do not chase aggressively ahead of Thursday NFP — high event risk. Consider taking partial profits on any pop into 30000 round level.
